Off Calendar Dec-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert R. Cross, Esq., 510.419.2231, for plaintiffs Elba Lucero and Lucero LLC.
Shane M. Cahill, Esq., 415.438.4594 and Jessica R. MacGregor, Esq., 415.397.2222, for defendants Dale Boutiette, Boutiette & Von Hermann, LLP and BVH Law Group, LLP.

RULING: Matter argued. At conclusion of argument, the court takes DEFENDANTS DALE BOUTIETTE, BOUTIETTE + VON HERMANN, LLP, BVH LAW GROUP, LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT under submission. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward #7077.

*****After hearing, the Court makes the following ruling:
The prevailing party is requested to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel on the top left of the first page of the proposed order. The order shall comply with Code of Civil Procedure 437c (g).

Motion for summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all issues. As to issues 1 through 3, Defendants shifted the burden by showing that the statute of limitations began running no later than April 13, 2007, and therefore expired before the tolling agreement was effective on April 16, 2008. However, Plaintiffs raised triable issues of fact regarding when Plaintiffs discovered the malpractice, when actual injury occurred, and when attorney Boutiette's representation ended. See plaintiff's UMF 1, 2, 4, 5, 6 (Bayer Decl. para 2, 3, 4, and 5); Lucero Decl. (2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7.)As to issues 4 and 5, Defendants failed to shift the burden. The Agreement to Toll Statute of Limitations (Boutiette Decl., Ex. A.) ('tolling agreement') was not executed on behalf of defendant Boutiette + Von Hermann, LLP. (UMF 91). However Boutiette + Von Hermann, LLP is included within the definition of 'attorneys' on this document, and the meaning of 'n/a' on the signature line for in unclear. Further, the 'Agreement to Extend Tolling Period,' also attached as Exhibit A, states that Boutiette + Von Hermann LLP was a party to the tolling agreement, continues to include Boutiette + Von Hermann as within the definition of 'attorneys,' and indicates it is the former name of BVH Law Group, LLP.

Defendants' objections to evidence:
Sustained: 1, 3, 11 (as to 'us' only), 12 (as to 'mother, sister' only), 17
The remaining objections are overruled.
(The Court will sign the separately prepared Order Re Defendants' Objections to Evidence in Support of Motion for Summary Judgment, or, in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication.)****
